rationale.R1.2: The section contains a probable internal logical inconsistency: Liu and Zhang (2019) are cited as reporting 91.2% AUROC at 50% coverage, yet RadAbstain's 87.4% at the same coverage is claimed as 'the strongest published result on this benchmark to date.' 87.4 < 91.2, making this a load-bearing coherence problem if accuracy review is the requested task.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple specific numerical claims (87.4% AUROC, 50% coverage, 91.2% prior-work figure, per-pathology gains on Pneumonia and Edema) are asserted without in-text citation of the supporting experiment or table—Table 2 is referenced but not described in enough detail to verify the claims here.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' commonly signals writing improvement; the section has several fluency opportunities (e.g., abrupt transitions between paragraphs, overloaded sentences in the prior-work paragraph), making polish a plausible load-bearing task.
